title: ceodes diandra
intro: Ceodes diandra, also known as the Indian Snakeroot, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. Its health benefits include relief from fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use in treating snake bites and other venomous stings. Ceodes diandra can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
